recommendations.description: Fix 2 rule(s) with critical configuration validation issues
recommendations.impact: CRITICAL - Ensures rules correctly validate compliance requirements

critical_issues.description: Rule name suggests authentication but checks data processing configuration (DataFormatConversionConfiguration, ProcessingConfiguration) instead of authentication

current_mapping.expected_value: Should check authentication configuration, not data processing

critical_issues.problem: Rule name suggests authentication but checks data processing configuration (DataFormatConversionConfiguration, ProcessingConfiguration) instead of authentication
critical_issues.impact: HIGH - Rule checks wrong fields, doesn't validate what name suggests
critical_issues.recommendation: Fix field paths to check actual authentication configuration, not data processing settings

critical_issues.description: Rule has hardcoded placeholder ARN (arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/KinesisFirehoseServiceRole) which will never match real resources. Also has contradictory checks (exists and equals specific ARN).
critical_issues.problem: Rule has hardcoded placeholder ARN (arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/KinesisFirehoseServiceRole) which will never match real resources. Also has contradictory checks (exists and equals specific ARN).
critical_issues.impact: CRITICAL - Hardcoded placeholder ARN will never match real resources, rule will always fail
critical_issues.recommendation: Remove hardcoded ARN check and fix contradictory logic. Should validate IAM role policies for least privilege or check role exists without specific ARN.

cross_service_analysis.note: Note: All methods used are kinesisfirehose service methods
cross_service_analysis.explanation: All python_methods (describe_delivery_stream) belong to kinesisfirehose service. No cross-service issues found.
cross_service_analysis.recommendation: No action needed - rules correctly use kinesisfirehose API methods

aws.kinesisfirehose.deliverystream.consu.issue: Checks data format conversion and processing configuration instead of consumer authentication

aws.kinesisfirehose.deliverystream.kines.issue: Checks hardcoded placeholder role ARN instead of least privilege, wrong field path (DestinationDescription doesn't exist)

aws.kinesisfirehose.deliverystream.loggi.issue: Field paths reference DeliveryStreamDescription wrapper that doesn't exist in emit structure, Destinations is an array

aws.kinesisfirehose.deliverystream.priva.issue: Field path references DestinationDescription which doesn't exist, Destinations is an array

aws.kinesisfirehose.deliverystream.kines.issue: Field path references ExtendedS3DestinationDescription at top level, but it's nested in Destinations array

summary.common_issue: Destinations array not properly handled - most rules reference DestinationDescription or top-level fields instead of Destinations array
summary.overall_status: FAIL - 5 out of 6 rules have critical logic/field path issues
